channel.title: JTF News Daily Digest
channel.itunes:author: JTF News
channel.description: Verified facts without opinion. Daily news digest following the JTF methodology: two unrelated sources, no adjectives, just facts. What happened, where, when, and how many. Nothing more.

item.title: JTF News - February 15, 2026
item.content:encoded: 0 verified facts for February 15, 2026.

item.title: JTF News - February 16, 2026
item.content:encoded: 0 verified facts for February 16, 2026.

item.title: JTF News - February 17, 2026
item.content:encoded: 0 verified facts for February 17, 2026.

item.title: JTF News - February 18, 2026
item.content:encoded: 0 verified facts for February 18, 2026.

item.title: JTF News - February 19, 2026
item.content:encoded: 0 verified facts for February 19, 2026.

item.title: JTF News - February 20, 2026
item.content:encoded: 0 verified facts for February 20, 2026.

item.title: JTF News - February 22, 2026
item.content:encoded:
5 verified facts for February 22, 2026.
- Russia and Ukraine reported overnight strikes on energy infrastructure. - U.S. Secret Service personnel shot and killed a man attempting to enter Mar-a-Lago. - The U.S. men's hockey team defeated Canada in overtime to win an Olympic gold medal. - The Mexican army killed drug lord Nemesio Oseguera Cervantes, known as 'El Mencho'. - Olympic cauldrons were extinguished in Milan and Cortina d'Ampezzo, signaling the end of the 2026 Winter Olympics.
JTF News: Two sources. No adjectives. Just facts.

item.title: JTF News - February 23, 2026
item.content:encoded:
13 verified facts for February 23, 2026.
- An armed man was killed after entering a secure perimeter of President Trump's residence, according to the Secret Service. - Mexican army reports death of drug trafficking organization leader Ismael Oseguera Cervantes. - Olympic cauldrons were extinguished in Milan and Cortina d'Ampezzo, marking the conclusion of the 2026 Winter Olympics. - President Trump imposes a temporary import duty. - North Korea's ruling party reelected Kim Jong Un as general secretary. - The United States and Iran are scheduled to hold nuclear talks in Geneva on Thursday. - New York implemented a citywide travel ban as a major blizzard affected the eastern United States. - Robert Mugabe's son has been charged with attempted murder in South Africa. - British police arrested Peter Mandelson, a former ambassador to the United States, in connection with an investigation into connections to Jeffrey Epstein. - The International Criminal Court opens hearings into former President Rodrigo Duterte. - The Mexican army killed cartel leader Nemesio Oseguera Cervantes, known as 'El Mencho.' - A son of Rob Reiner pleaded not guilty to charges related to the deaths of his parents. - The State Department ordered nonessential U.S. diplomats to leave Lebanon.
JTF News: Two sources. No adjectives. Just facts.

item.title: JTF News - February 24, 2026
item.content:encoded:
12 verified facts for February 24, 2026.
- A winter storm is affecting travel across the northeastern U.S. - The U.S. military conducted a strike on a suspected drug boat in the Caribbean, resulting in 3 deaths. - President Trump will deliver a State of the Union speech. - President Volodymyr Zelensky addressed the European Parliament as Ukraine marked four years since the Russian invasion began. - Mexico deployed thousands of soldiers in response to violence following the death of a drug lord. - Russell Brand pleaded not guilty to additional rape and sexual assault charges. - Nancy Guthrie's family offers $1 million reward for information regarding missing mother. - The United States partially evacuated its embassy in Beirut. - Prime Minister of Senegal proposes doubling jail terms for same-sex acts. - President Emmanuel Macron of France accepted the resignation of the Louvre director following a jewel theft in October. - France stated its ministers will not meet with the U.S. ambassador until the ambassador responds to a summons. - The International Criminal Court opens hearings into former President Rodrigo Duterte of the Philippines.
JTF News: Two sources. No adjectives. Just facts.

item.title: JTF News - February 25, 2026
item.content:encoded:
11 verified facts for February 25, 2026.
- Floods and landslides in Brazil resulted in at least 20 deaths. - Ukraine marks four years of war with Russia. - President Trump is scheduled to deliver a State of the Union address. - President Trump delivers State of the Union address. - Australia police make arrests in connection with the death of a grandfather who was mistakenly kidnapped. - Chancellor Friedrich Merz of Germany travels to Beijing. - Trial begins for Brazil politicians accused of ordering lawmakers' murders. - Japan announced plans to deploy missiles on an island near Taiwan by 2031. - Brazil politicians convicted for ordering murder of Rio activist councillor. - Cuban coast guard personnel killed 4 people in a US-flagged speedboat. - Ukrainian officials will meet with envoys representing President Trump in Geneva for talks.
JTF News: Two sources. No adjectives. Just facts.

item.title: JTF News - February 26, 2026
item.content:encoded: 0 verified facts for February 26, 2026.

item.title: JTF News - February 28, 2026
item.content:encoded:
13 verified facts for February 28, 2026.
- At least 55 Ghanaians have been killed in the Russia-Ukraine war, according to a Ghanaian minister. - Sweden reported that a Russian drone flew near a French aircraft carrier. - former President Bill Clinton stated to lawmakers that he 'did nothing wrong' regarding Jeffrey Epstein. - A tram derailed in Milan, resulting in two deaths and dozens of injuries. - President Trump suggested the U.S. could have a 'friendly takeover of Cuba'. - President Trump announced a ban on U.S. government use of Anthropic. - At least 11 people died after a military plane carrying banknotes crashed in Bolivia. - Israel announced it conducted a military strike against Iran. - Israel declared a state of emergency and stated it launched a strike against Iran. - Paramount is set for a $111 billion acquisition by Warner Bros. after Netflix withdrew its bid. - President Trump stated the US began 'major combat operations' against Iran. - Canadian Foreign Minister Chrystya Freeland urged all Canadians in Iran to shelter in place as the U.S. and Israel launched attacks. - Iran states it has conducted strikes on a U.S. military base in Bahrain and launched strikes in the region.
JTF News: Two sources. No adjectives. Just facts.

item.title: JTF News - February 27, 2026
item.content:encoded:
6 verified facts for February 27, 2026.
- At least 55 Ghanaians killed in Russia-Ukraine war, according to a Ghanaian government minister. - Sweden states that a drone jammed near a French aircraft carrier was Russian. - former President Bill Clinton stated to lawmakers that he 'did nothing wrong' regarding Epstein. - A tram derailed in Milan, resulting in two deaths and dozens of injuries. - President Trump suggested the U.S. could have a 'friendly takeover of Cuba.' - Pakistan's Defense Minister stated that Pakistan is in open war with Afghanistan following recent strikes.
JTF News: Two sources. No adjectives. Just facts.

item.title: JTF News - March 2, 2026
item.content:encoded:
9 verified facts for March 2, 2026.
- At least 20 people died when a military plane carrying banknotes crashed in Bolivia. - Three U.S. troops were killed and five others were seriously wounded during Iran attacks, according to the military. - At least 148 people are dead following a reported strike on a school, according to Iranian authorities. - The U.S. stated that Kuwait downed 3 American jets during Iranian attacks, with all crew members safely ejecting. - Israel and Hezbollah exchange fire in southern and eastern Lebanon. - President Emmanuel Macron of France stated France will increase its nuclear arsenal. - President Trump stated the US acted on its 'last, best chance' to hit Iran. - 169 people killed in a village in a remote area of South Sudan after an insurgent raid. - Oil prices rise following attacks on ships near the Strait of Hormuz.
JTF News: Two sources. No adjectives. Just facts.

item.title: JTF News - March 3, 2026
item.content:encoded:
8 verified facts for March 3, 2026.
- President Emmanuel Macron of France stated France will increase its nuclear arsenal and allow temporary deployment of nuclear-armed jets to European allies. - Israel conducted strikes on Beirut and Tehran. - The United States imposed sanctions on the Rwandan military over fighting in eastern Congo. - U.S. embassy in Saudi Arabia was hit by Iranian drones. - The State Department urged U.S. citizens to leave more than a dozen Middle Eastern countries. - President Trump meets with Friedrich Merz, Chancellor of Germany. - President Trump stated Iran is 'in for a lot of hurt' during an Oval Office address. - The father of the accused Georgia school shooter was convicted of murder.
JTF News: Two sources. No adjectives. Just facts.

item.title: JTF News - March 4, 2026
item.content:encoded:
9 verified facts for March 4, 2026.
- The father of the accused Georgia school shooter was convicted of murder. - President Emmanuel Macron of France ordered France's nuclear-powered aircraft carrier to move from the Baltic Sea to the Mediterranean. - The Pentagon identified 4 of 6 U.S. soldiers killed in a drone strike in Kuwait. - US and Ecuador forces launch operation to fight drug trafficking. - An 8-year-old Australian girl died in a snowmobile accident in Japan. - Israel conducted airstrikes in Lebanon and Iran. - A Greek court upheld convictions of leaders of a neo-Nazi party. - The Senate votes on a war powers resolution following U.S.-Israeli strikes on Iran. - Sri Lanka recovered 87 bodies from an Iranian warship sunk off its coast by a U.S. submarine.
JTF News: Two sources. No adjectives. Just facts.

item.title: JTF News - March 5, 2026
item.content:encoded:
9 verified facts for March 5, 2026.
- The Senate voted against a measure to constrain President Trump's authorities in Iran. - The Pentagon identified six U.S. soldiers killed in an Iran conflict. - Cuba experiences widespread power outages amid fuel shortage. - A blackout affects millions of people in Cuba. - Kim Jong Un visited a warship and oversaw a missile launch in North Korea. - Israel conducted strikes in Beirut. - Israel issued an evacuation order for Beirut's southern suburbs. - President Trump announced that Governor Kristi Noem will be replaced as Secretary of Homeland Security. - Azerbaijan reports Iran conducted strikes on its territory.
JTF News: Two sources. No adjectives. Just facts.

item.title: JTF News - March 6, 2026
item.content:encoded:
6 verified facts for March 6, 2026.
- The DR Congo government reported 200 deaths from a landslide at a mine in a rebel-held area. - Israel launches attacks on Tehran and Beirut. - A Canadian tourist died by drowning in Australia following a dingo attack, according to authorities. - Canada is signing an agreement with the European Union to enhance a free trade deal. - President Trump stated that Governor Kristi Noem will be replaced as Secretary of Homeland Security. - Oil prices reached $90 USD per barrel for the first time in 2 years.
JTF News: Two sources. No adjectives. Just facts.

item.title: JTF News - March 7, 2026
item.content:encoded:
4 verified facts for March 7, 2026.
- Indonesia's government announced a plan to ban social media access for users under 16 years old. - A Russian strike on an apartment block in Kharkiv killed seven people. - At least six people died after tornadoes swept across Michigan and Oklahoma, according to officials. - A bombing at a nightclub in Peru injured 33 people, including minors.
JTF News: Two sources. No adjectives. Just facts.

item.title: JTF News - March 8, 2026
item.content:encoded:
8 verified facts for March 8, 2026.
- At least six people died after tornadoes in Michigan and Oklahoma, according to officials. - Russia conducted strikes on a Kharkiv apartment block in Ukraine. - President Trump encouraged Latin American leaders to use military action to help the United States fight cartels. - A bombing at a nightclub in Peru injured 33 people, including minors. - An explosion was reported outside the U.S. embassy in Oslo. - Israel conducted strikes on oil storage sites near Tehran, Iran. - A search for missing Flight MH370 concluded without locating the aircraft 12 years after its disappearance. - Iranian state TV reports Mojtaba Khamenei named successor to the supreme leader.
JTF News: Two sources. No adjectives. Just facts.

item.title: JTF News - March 9, 2026
item.content:encoded:
7 verified facts for March 9, 2026.
- An explosion occurred at the U.S. embassy in Oslo, and Norway police are investigating whether it was terrorism. - A Russian missile struck an apartment building in Kharkiv, Ukraine, resulting in at least 10 deaths. - An explosion occurred in front of a synagogue in Liege, Belgium. - Heavy rains and flooding killed at least 23 people in Nairobi. - An attempted attack with explosives in New York City is under investigation. - President Trump called on Australia to provide asylum to Iranian women's soccer players. - Canada commits nearly $1 billion to drone and airborne defence research.
JTF News: Two sources. No adjectives. Just facts.

item.title: JTF News - March 10, 2026
item.content:encoded:
10 verified facts for March 10, 2026.
- President Trump called on Australia to provide asylum to Iranian women's soccer players. - Two men are charged in connection with a nail bomb thrown at a protest in New York. - President Trump had a call with President Putin to discuss Iran, according to the Kremlin. - Iran stated it will fight 'as long as needed' after President Trump stated the war will end 'soon'. - US authorities searched Jeffrey Epstein's former ranch. - Five Iran national team players were granted asylum in Australia. - Volkswagen plans to reduce workforce by 50,000 positions by 2030. - Canadian police are investigating reports of shots fired at the U.S. consulate in Toronto. - Environment Canada warns of a freezing rain storm approaching southern Quebec. - The United Nations states that Russia's deportation of Ukrainian children constitutes a crime against humanity.
JTF News: Two sources. No adjectives. Just facts.
